overview: 4c Ponza is a four-color midrange deck that leverages land destruction to disrupt opponents while maintaining board presence with efficient creatures and token generation. By integrating blue into the traditional W/R/G foundation, the archetype gains additional card advantage and tempo tools to outlast aggressive strategies and race control decks. The core loop revolves around trading lands for creatures, using Utopia Sprawl to stabilize, and closing games with overwhelming combat pressure.
win_conditions: Overwhelm the opponent with a board of creatures while their lands are destroyed
Deal direct damage through land destruction effects combined with combat
Outvalue the opponent in the late game using Utopia Sprawl token generation

keep_criteria: Hands with early land destruction like Thermokarst or Avenging Hunter
Mana base that supports at least two colors by turn two
Cheap creatures or Utopia Sprawl to establish early board presence
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one land destruction spell (Thermokarst/Ruination), and a turn-2 threat or interaction.
Prioritize hands that can play a land and a spell on turn 1, or two lands and a spell on turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or land destruction
Mana screws with too many lands or too few
High-cost cards without early support or draw
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or 2+ lands with only removal and no threats/land destruction.

early_game: Play lands and cheap threats to establish board presence
Use Thermokarst or Avenging Hunter to destroy key opponent lands
Prioritize disrupting aggressive strategies before they gain momentum
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or cheap interaction if available, or hold for a turn 2 land destruction.
Turn 2: Cast Thermokarst to disrupt opponent's mana, or develop a threat like Avenging Hunter.
Prioritize fetching/shocking for optimal land drops.
Against aggro, hold removal for early threats
mid_game: Maintain board control while generating value with Utopia Sprawl
Sequence land destruction to hit lands before opponent casts high-cost spells
Use blue cards for card draw or removal to stabilize against board wipes
Conserve mana for interaction until you establish board control.
Use cantrips to dig for answers or threats.
Avoid overcommitting to Utopia Sprawl without a threat to pump.
Manage graveyard by tracking recursion threats
hold graveyard hate if the opponent is building toward it.
Commit threats only when you have removal or land destruction to protect them or answer the opponent's response.
late_game: Flood the board with Treefolk tokens from Utopia Sprawl
Race control decks by pushing damage while their mana is disrupted
Trade efficiently to preserve lands and close out the game
Utopia Sprawl + Avenging Hunter is the primary win condition, often supported by direct damage or discard to clear the path.
Secondary finishers include resilient midrange threats and pump spells that close out games in one or two turns.
Go all-in when you have board control, the opponent has no answers to your pump, or you can clear their blockers with removal/damage.
Commit when you've disrupted their mana and they're on a resource deficit.

key_cards.name: Thermokarst
key_cards.role: Instant-speed land destruction and direct damage
key_cards.notes: Hold for when opponent plays a key land or before combat to maximize disruption and damage output

key_cards.name: Avenging Hunter
key_cards.role: Cheap creature with land destruction on death
key_cards.notes: Use as an early threat or sacrifice to trigger its death ability, targeting opponent's most impactful land

key_cards.name: Utopia Sprawl
key_cards.role: Land untap effect and token generator
key_cards.notes: Enter tapped to untap a land for spells, then repeatedly generate 1/1 Treefolk tokens to outvalue and close games

sequencing_tips: Hold Thermokarst until after opponent commits to a land or before combat to maximize impact
Trigger Avenging Hunter's death ability strategically, often by trading it into a removal spell or sacrificing it
Use Utopia Sprawl's untap ability to cast multiple spells in a turn or to generate tokens for combat tricks
Prioritize destroying opponent's nonbasic lands or lands that enable their key strategies
Utopia Sprawl + Avenging Hunter is a powerful but fragile combo
protect it with removal or discard.
Thermokarst is your best early game spell against ramp/affinity but useless against non-land strategies.
Don't overcommit to land destruction in control mirrors.
Watch for graveyard recursion in Jund/Wildfire.
Practice sequencing cantrips to dig for answers without flooding your board.

matchups.advice: Play land destruction early to slow their mana and use cheap blockers to survive the initial rush
Disrupt their mana base early and race them before they can assemble board wipes or win conditions
Race them by maintaining board presence and using land destruction to disrupt their curve

Use cantrips to dig past counters, cast spells in response to their counters, or use uncounterable threats. Hold up mana for protection, but don't overextend. Consider discard to remove key pieces before they can be countered.
Aggressive land destruction is key. Prioritize Thermokarst on turn 1-2. Use discard to remove key ramp pieces. Stabilize with early threats and removal. Don't let them get to 4+ lands.

Win through card advantage and efficient trades. Prioritize keeping removal for their threats. Use discard to disrupt their game plan. Win the resource war by out-grinding them with better sequencing and sideboard cards.

common_threats: Early aggressive creatures that apply pressure before land destruction can stabilize
Board wipe spells that clear your creature board and tokens
Card draw and value engines that outpace your resource generation
Watch for mana acceleration (Elves, Affinity), combo win conditions (Madness, Terror), and control win cons (Jund, Grixis).
Key tells: opponent playing multiple lands early, drawing extra cards, or setting up enchantments/auras.
Track their graveyard for recursion or mill threats.
